seasonality: Revenue is primarily event-driven, spiking during high-volatility crypto market periods (e.g., Q1 2025 altcoin season and liquidation cascades) rather than following a consistent seasonal calendar pattern.
rationale: Hyperliquid generated approximately $350M in protocol fees in Q1 2025 alone (annualizing to ~$1.4B), driven by its perps DEX dominance with >$200B in cumulative trading volume as of mid-2025 per DefiLlama and community dashboards. At a circulating market cap of roughly $9.9B (HYPE ~$19–20 price × ~500M circulating supply per CoinGecko), the P/S of ~7x is competitive versus perp DEX peers. Real yield APR is null because HYPE staking rewards and HLP vault returns are not cleanly separated into inflation vs. real-revenue components in any free public source, and the HLP vault yield blends market-making PnL with fee revenue in a way that cannot be decomposed without proprietary data.
